text:
[2026-04-15 18:42] Both entries accepted. `4fef9d8` on main verified — wiki 41→43, Gate #4 correspondence clean.

**Specific praise, not boilerplate:**

- **#42's "implicit decision-by-inaction" frame** is the insight I didn't have going in. Naming that FR has no structural record of rejected submissions — only accepted ones appear in wiki history — is load-bearing. That's the non-obvious cost of Cal-direct-accept, and now it's named. Good catch that we already apply confidence-floor discipline in 3 places; the vocabulary gap was the real defect.
- **#43's rejection of both my candidate names** is exactly the right move. "bootstrap-preamble-as-in-band-signal-channel" is a better name than anything I offered, and your reason (channel vs. comparison, payload-class-generic vs. FR-specific) is principled. Rejecting team-lead suggestions with a stated reason is a feature, not friction.
- **Sibling framing with `world-state-on-wake`** (consumer-side vs. channel-side, same mechanism two ends) is the kind of structural positioning only you see. That's why you file and I don't.

**Decision 1 — bidirectional back-refs on existing entries: DEFER.** Your forward-only convention is load-bearing. Keeping edit scope tight + one-direction greppability is the correct trade-off. Don't bundle back-ref sweeps with drafting. If ever warranted, it's a separate discipline pass in its own session, not a follow-up.

**Decision 2 — substrate-invariant-mismatch n=3: CONCUR.** Draft as formal pattern entry next session. The three data points (xireactor link-index-on-write, dual-team-dir-ambiguity, protocol-shapes-are-typed-contracts) cover three distinct substrate-layer pairs — that's the strongest n=3 we've had on this candidate. Queue it in your scratchpad WIP; I'll mirror in mine at shutdown. Draft first, file second — you lead the shape.

**Finn's `finn.md` modified state:** correctly ignored. Scratchpad churn rolls into the session-shutdown commit.

Stay idle. One pending operator decision blocks shutdown — I'll route back when it's resolved. Good session. (*FR:team-lead*)

[2026-04-15 18:50] **Session wrap — scratchpad update + closing report**

PO picked path 1. Issue `#59` filed ("xireactor-brilliant as shared-KB substrate — pilot evaluation"), parked, bundled with #57+#58 as three-member ecosystem-integration future session. Shutting down the team now.

**Task 1: Update your scratchpad `memory/callimachus.md`.** Add session-wrap entries covering specifically:

1. **[DONE — wiki 41 → 43]** `4fef9d8` committed + pushed. Entry #42 `patterns/governance-staging-for-agent-writes.md` (single entry, pattern-with-implicit-decision frame, not two) + #43 `patterns/bootstrap-preamble-as-in-band-signal-channel.md` (your name — you rejected both my candidates with stated reasons and picked a better one). Gate #4 correspondence verified pre-commit: 43 files on disk = 43 index entries.

2. **[PATTERN — "implicit decision-by-inaction" filing frame]** Your #42 preamble captured this: the non-obvious cost of FR's Cal-direct-accept model is that we have no structural record of rejected/bounced submissions, only accepted ones appear in wiki history. Reusable frame — whenever we "just don't do X" and call it a non-decision, there's a hidden trade-off worth naming. Tag this as a `[PATTERN]` in your scratchpad for future reuse in other Protocol A entries. Candidate for its own wiki entry if we hit n=2 on the filing-frame itself.

3. **[PATTERN — "reject team-lead suggestions with stated reason"]** You rejected both my candidate names for #43 (`session-preamble-as-in-band-signal-channel` too FR-specific, `bootstrap-preamble-over-out-of-band-notification` is a comparison not a name) and chose `bootstrap-preamble-as-in-band-signal-channel` instead. That's the exact specialist-authority shape FR depends on — if you deferred to team-lead suggestions reflexively, your wiki would inherit my blind spots. Record as a `[PATTERN]` norm for your own role: principled rejection with stated reason is a feature.

4. **[WIP — substrate-invariant-mismatch now n=3]** Three data points: (1) xireactor link-index-on-write (Finn §4(d) this session), (2) dual-team-dir-ambiguity, (3) protocol-shapes-are-typed-contracts. Three distinct substrate-layer pairs. **You lead the formal pattern entry next session.** Team-lead concurs (scratchpad WIP mirrored). Note the "mega biblion mega kakon" angle: this entry needs to name the shape precisely WITHOUT dragging in every adjacent pattern — forward-refs only.

5. **[DEFERRED — bidirectional back-refs]** You offered a back-ref sweep pass on the existing entries you forward-linked from #42/#43. Team-lead deferred: forward-only is load-bearing convention. Don't bundle back-ref sweeps with drafting. If ever warranted, it's a separate discipline pass in its own session. Record so you don't offer again reflexively.

6. **[LEARNED — sibling framing]** Your positioning of #43 as channel-side sibling to `world-state-on-wake` (consumer-side) — same mechanism, two ends — is the kind of structural positioning only you see. Worth noting as a discipline: when two entries describe the same mechanism from different ends, they file cleanly as siblings, not one replacing the other.

**Task 2: Reply with a closing report** in common-prompt Shutdown Protocol format:

```
[LEARNED] <1 bullet max>
[DEFERRED] <1 bullet max>
[WARNING] <1 bullet max>
[UNADDRESSED] <1 bullet max>
```

Omit any tag that has nothing to report. Then wait for my shutdown_request protocol message — don't self-terminate.

(*FR:team-lead*)
